aliases: アイスグリーンティー

japanese: アイスグリーンティー

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard glass of iced green tea is approximately 300 ml (300 g), providing roughly 3 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Tea, green, brewed, regular (SR Legacy ID 171917)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture — Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed green tea (regular, no additives, FDC ID 171917) used as the primary nutrient reference. Values represent plain brewed green tea without sugar or milk, consistent with the iced preparation described. Input per_100g values from grabfood were cross-checked against USDA and found broadly consistent; USDA SR Legacy values adopted as the approved-source anchor per Project Veritas source policy for Japanese-cuisine beverages.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care — American Diabetes Association peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed green tea contains negligible carbohydrates (0.27 g/100 g per USDA FDC SR Legacy ID 171917). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates and no measurable starch or sugar load. The peer-reviewed International GI Tables (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) confirm that non-caloric and near-zero-carbohydrate beverages such as plain tea do not carry a meaningful GI value. glycemic_index is therefore set to null and gi_category to 'not_applicable' per Project Veritas q4 gate rules for negligible-carbohydrate foods. Glycemic load per 300 g serving is 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium (1 mg/100 g); no added sugar, saturated fat, or alcohol
hypertension.guidance: Plain unsweetened iced green tea is appropriate for individuals with hypertension; its negligible sodium content and catechin-mediated endothelial benefits support blood pressure management, provided no sugar or high-sodium additives are added at the point of service.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrates (0.27 g/100 g) and zero sugar; EGCG catechins have evidence-based insulin-sensitising properties
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain unsweetened iced green tea is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its negligible carbohydrate and zero sugar content; green tea catechins (EGCG) have demonstrated insulin-sensitising effects in peer-reviewed research, and the beverage should be consumed without added sweeteners to preserve glycaemic benefit.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero trans fat, zero cholesterol; EGCG polyphenols reduce LDL oxidation and support endothelial function
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain unsweetened iced green tea is cardioprotective; its zero saturated fat, zero trans fat, and zero cholesterol profile combined with EGCG polyphenols that reduce LDL oxidation and improve endothelial function make it a suitable daily beverage for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk, provided it is consumed without added sugar or cream.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: EGCG polyphenols inhibit pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Plain unsweetened iced green tea may benefit individuals with rheumatoid arthritis through EGCG-mediated inhibition of pro-inflammatory cytokines; caffeine content (~30–50 mg per 240 ml) is within safe limits, but patients on methotrexate should consult their prescriber regarding caffeine interactions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Green Tea is a refreshing cold beverage prepared by brewing green tea leaves (Camellia sinensis) in hot water and then chilling the resulting infusion over ice. The drink originates from Japan, where green tea (ryokucha) is a cultural staple, and is widely served across Southeast Asia including Singapore as a restaurant and café beverage. The flavour profile is light, slightly grassy, mildly astringent, and subtly vegetal, with no sweetness in its plain unsweetened form. Nutritionally, plain iced green tea is extremely low in calories (approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy), contains negligible carbohydrates (0.27 g/100 ml), zero sugar, zero fat, and minimal sodium (1 mg/100 ml). It is rich in polyphenolic catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), which have well-documented antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and metabolic benefits. The beverage is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease when consumed without added sugar or sweeteners. A standard serving is approximately 300 ml (1 glass), providing roughly 3 kcal. The glycaemic load per serving is clinically negligible. Iced green tea is naturally vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal. Caffeine content is approximately 30–50 mg per 240 ml serving, which is well within safe limits for most adults.
common_user_queries: Is iced green tea good for diabetes?
How many calories in iced green tea?
Does iced green tea have sugar?
Is iced green tea suitable for hypertension?
What is the glycemic index of green tea?
How much caffeine is in iced green tea?
Is iced green tea good for weight loss?
Can I drink iced green tea if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (brewed green tea, no additives, FDC ID 171917), an approved primary source under Project Veritas source policy for Japanese-cuisine beverages. The food is a simple, well-characterised beverage with minimal preparation variability in its plain unsweetened form. GI inapplicability confirmed by peer-reviewed International GI Tables (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care).
notes: Fat_g is naturally zero: plain brewed green tea contains no fat by composition, confirmed per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171917). Sugar_g is naturally zero: no sweeteners are added in the plain preparation; confirmed per USDA SR Legacy. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero: the tea leaves are not consumed, only the brewed liquid; confirmed per USDA SR Legacy. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — plant-based beverage with no animal-derived components). Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, and polyunsaturated_fat_g are all 0.0 (naturally zero — no fat present in brewed green tea, confirmed per USDA SR Legacy). Trans_fat_g is 0.0 (naturally zero — no fat present). Calcium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg are reported as 0.0 per USDA SR Legacy for brewed green tea; these represent confirmed negligible or below-detection-threshold values in the brewed liquid. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.27 g/100 g); glycemic_index set to null and gi_category to 'not_applicable' per Project Veritas q4 gate rules; inapplicability supported by Atkinson et al. (2008) International GI Tables, Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal). Input source listed 'https://foodo.sg/' as the HPB Singapore URL — this was not used as a primary nutrient source; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy was used instead as the approved source for this Japanese-cuisine beverage. Country of origin in input was listed as Singapore (serving context); region of origin corrected to Japan as the cultural origin of green tea. Input per_100g values from grabfood (energy 1.67 kcal, protein 0.17 g, carbohydrate 0.33 g, sodium 1.67 mg) were superseded by USDA SR Legacy values as the approved-source anchor.
